title: Medicine thinks Gen Z is too soft. It's wrong
summary: Frantz M. Berthaud, an oncology services executive at the University Medical Center of El Paso, argues against dismissing Gen Z physicians as "too soft" and makes a case for health care executives listening to young doctors instead. The piece opens with a personal anecdote about being surprised by how young and untraditional his last physician was, using it to frame a broader reflection on how quietly the medical profession has changed.

entities.name: University Medical Center of El Paso

analysis_notes: Full article text is behind STAT paywall. Analysis based on headline, subtitle (~50 words of lead-in text), and metadata only. Author makes an opinion argument about valuing Gen Z/young physicians; no quantifiable findings, data, or biotech-specific developments are evident from the accessible portion.
